source.title: Prioritized memory access explains planning and hippocampal replay

confidence_rationale: Strong computational model with clear normative derivation showing replay prioritized by gain (prediction-error dependent) x need. The gain term captures MECH-205's surprise-gating; the generative capacity (composing novel sequences from pieces) maps onto MECH-205's counterfactual variations. Minor mapping gap: the model prioritizes by expected policy improvement rather than raw prediction error magnitude, which is a more refined criterion than MECH-205's formulation.
failure_signatures: The model prioritizes by expected BEHAVIORAL gain, not raw prediction error -- replay of a surprise that cannot change policy is not prioritized, which is a more restrictive criterion than MECH-205's surprise buffer.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Replay utility is the product of a gain term (prioritizing states behind the agent when an unexpected outcome is encountered) and a need term (prioritizing states ahead that are imminently relevant). Replay can compose novel behavioral sequences from separately experienced pieces.
mapping.ree_translation: The gain term maps onto MECH-205's surprise-gating: high prediction error increases replay priority. The need term adds a forward-looking component MECH-205 does not explicitly model. The compositional sequence generation maps onto MECH-205's generative counterfactual variations via E2. The implicit termination (utility falls below threshold) maps onto MECH-205's convergence criterion.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mattar & Daw's gain is policy-relevant surprise (would the update change behaviour?), not raw prediction error. MECH-205's surprise buffer uses prediction error magnitude directly, which is a less selective criterion. The model is for planning replay (awake), while MECH-205 targets offline sleep replay -- the prioritization logic may differ between awake and sleep states.
mapping.source_context: Computational reinforcement learning model (DYNA framework) validated against rodent place cell replay data in spatial navigation tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Nature Neuroscience, highly cited normative framework. Mapping fidelity reduced because the gain x need product is more refined than raw surprise; transfer risk low because the framework is general RL, applicable to any replay system.
